creator: webbstanley@gmail.com
text:
I am new to Freebsd I hope I do this right. If not I apoligize.

I tried to install gnat. after downloading what was needed Thhis error message apeared.

DarkStar# cd /usr/ports/lang/gnat
DarkStar# make install clean
===>  Building for gnat-2005
(cd /usr/ports/lang/gnat/work/gcc-34;  PATH=/usr/ports/lang/gnat/work/adaboot/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/games:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/X11R6/bin:/root/bin  GCC_EXEC_PREFIX=/usr/ports/lang/gnat/work/adaboot/lib/gcc-lib/  ADA_INCLUDE_PATH=/usr/ports/lang/gnat/work/adaboot/lib/gcc-lib/adainclude  ADA_OBJECTS_PATH=/usr/ports/lang/gnat/work/adaboot/lib/gcc-lib/adalib gmake CC=adagcc )
Configuring in intl
loading cache ./config.cache
checking for non-GNU ld... /usr/bin/ld
checking if the linker (/usr/bin/ld) is GNU ld... yes
checking how to run the C preprocessor... /lib/cpp
checking for inttypes.h... no
checking for stdint.h... no
checking for unsigned long long... no
checking for shared library run path origin... done
checking whether make sets ${MAKE}... yes
checking for a BSD compatible install... /usr/bin/install -c
checking whether NLS is requested... no
checking for gcc... adagcc
checking whether the C compiler (adagcc -g -O2 ) works... no
configure: error: installation or configuration problem: C compiler cannot create executables.
gmake: *** [configure-intl] Error 1
*** Error code 2

Stop in /usr/ports/lang/gnat.
DarkStar#
